entry.title: Re: Asset Depreciation value changed in the middle of the...
entry.content:
Dear Mark,

Good Day.

There is no other phase on the Depreciation key.

Regards,

Shreekumar

entry.title: Register Now – Q3 EC User Group Meeting – Sept 24th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here to receive the event invite and login details.

Q3 Employee Central User Group Meeting

Tuesday, September 24th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Employee Central User Group is customer led by Corning and PepsiCo. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Employee Central customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from EC experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Register Now – Q3 EC User Group Meeting – Sept 24th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here to receive the event invite and login details.

Q3 Employee Central User Group Meeting

Tuesday, September 24th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Employee Central User Group is customer led by Corning and PepsiCo. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Employee Central customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from EC experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Capacity planning background processing
entry.content:
Hi my intention is to setup Capacity planning through CM40 for the background job. It is not a problem and I  can do it considering standard profiles.

The problem is: I needed to execute the job for Plant, Work center and Material. Standard selection profiles allow to execute only for Plant and Work center and some other fields, but not for Material. So I would like to create new Set:

 

 

If I look into existing valid set, I could see that some value is assigned to field:

But if I want to create own set using MATNR field I see that there is no value:

 

 

 

So when I use my own set with my own selection profile, it is not shown on selection screen in CM40:

The question is. Should I add some new value to the field MATNR. And after that some development should be done to consider new value? Or maybe there is some standard way to handle it?

If development should be done, then where it should be enhanced? 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #13
entry.content: This is quite early for US based resources. Will this session be recorded and made available to those registered?

entry.title: Re: How to create a Notification or a Situation when a In...
entry.content: Thanks Angelika for the standard way approach to configure the Manage Situation types , but here I am unable to configure or find out a way ' when ever a user changed a Incoterms or any other field in SAP Sales order ( VA02) then how to configure a notification to the user/email about this change . Seems there might be a possible solution to achieve my requirement. Thanks

entry.title: Group Reporting - FS items
entry.content: If there are transactions posted before the GL account has been mapped to an FS item, can the FS item be retrospectively added.

entry.title: SAP SAC have a data streaming functionality
entry.content:
HI 

Does SAP SAC have a data streaming functionality so that any change of the source table (new or change record) that is replicated in SAP DSP is  automatically pops up in SAP SAC report without manual refresh?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP on IBM i: New PTF Groups trigger IBM i administrator actions
entry.content:
Last week, IBM i 7.5 TR4 was released along with Db2 for i PTF Group SF99950 Level 7, as well as IBM i 7.4 TR 10 along with Db2 for i PTF Group SF99704 Level 28. You can get a quick overview over the enhancements for IBM i 7.5 at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-i-75-tr4-enhancements and for IBM i 7.4 at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-i-74-tr10-enhancements. A general entry point for the various Technology Refreshes with their enhancements can be found at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/node/1119129.

Attention: To further protect IBM i against vulnerabilities, the current PTF groups contain fixes that remove *PUBLIC authority *USE from some operating system commands. This may result in error SQL0552 for SQL statement CREATE TRIGGER, or error SQL0443 when accessing the system views PTF_INFO or GROUP_PTF_INFO. To avoid functional problems, it is advised that you grant *USE authority for user profile R3GROUP to these commands. To do so, sign on as QSECOFR or a user with similar authorities and execute the following commands:

GRTOBJAUT OBJ(QSYS/ADDPFTRG) OBJTYPE(*CMD) USER(R3GROUP) AUT(*USE)
GRTOBJAUT OBJ(QSYS/DSPPTF) OBJTYPE(*CMD) USER(R3GROUP) AUT(*USE)
GRTOBJAUT OBJ(QSYS/WRKPTFGRP) OBJTYPE(*CMD) USER(R3GROUP) AUT(*USE)

The SAP Support Required PTF Lists, formerly known as Information APARs, have been updated to include these new levels. The PTF lists can be found at the following links:

IBM i 7.5: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-75
IBM i 7.4: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-74
IBM i 7.3: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-73

If you want to download the PTF lists to your SAP systems with the help of a program, take a look at our sample program and blog entry from July 18th, 2022.

We recommend that you apply the current PTF levels for preventive maintenance whenever you are performing your next scheduled maintenance.

entry.title: Re: Variant configuration issue in production order
entry.content:
How to fetch sales order classification into production order till sub-assembly level?

Answer :

Strategy groups

 

 

Strategy assignment with Reqmt type of Cust req

 

Strategy details

 

Requirements classes

 

 

Requirement Types

 

 

 

 

 

Account Assignment Categories

 

Strategy Z0 (MTS)

 

Strategy assignment with Reqs type of customer Reqmt

 

 

 

Strategy Z0 Details

 

Requirement Type assign to ReqCl

 

Requirement Classes

 

 

 

Master data

Also if you are using material type FERT & HALB then you have to activate the check box "Material configure"(Basic data 2) in FERT as well as HALB (sub-assembly) with the same master data.

And you have to create a "Configuration Profile for the Materials " using CU41 for both FERT & HALB.

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation between asset subledger and general le...
entry.content:
I wrote a 4 part blog series on this very topic.  It's frequently the case, particularly in ECC, that this happens.  As more customers look to upgrade to S/4HANA, it's one of the first things I check to ensure that the data conversion will be accurate.

 

https://serioconsulting.com/blog/tag/reconciliation/

https://serioconsulting.com/blog/how-to-reconcile-fixed-assets-to-the-g-l-part-1/

https://serioconsulting.com/blog/how-to-reconcile-fixed-assets-to-the-g-l-part-2/

https://serioconsulting.com/blog/how-to-reconcile-fixed-assets-to-the-g-l-part-3/

https://serioconsulting.com/blog/how-to-reconcile-fixed-assets-to-the-g-l-part-4/

entry.title: Group Reporting - FS items best practice
entry.content: Does anyone know if there is a best practice file available for mapping the SAP standard CoA to FS items ?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to call a fix URL from IW52 trasnactio...
entry.content:
Could you please provide a link to the thread?

Does it matter what is behind the url? I have not get a clear idea about it yet from the customer.

entry.title: Re: Down payment with Resource related billing not consid...
entry.content: Hi Lakshmipathi, If you have gone through my post, we have gone through all notes 858702, 858703 and 983961 which SAP has given and completed required configuration post. Note which you mentioned is part of that. We would like to know why we are not getting desired output. If anything else is expected ?

entry.title: Re: Unable to access SAP BAS in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi,

 Can you check trying this?

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/appstudio-onboarding.html

entry.title: Extending "Stock - Mulitple Material" from Custon Field Logic App
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I need to add the purchase value field to the MMIM_MULTIPLE_MATERIAL_SRV service.
I would like to know if anyone has done this before? If so, how did you do it? Were you able to use any field already available in the list below?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: AUC Value is negative
entry.content: You should not disable this on AUC, see note 19048 - AA617 with credit memo on fixed asset / AuC settlement

entry.title: Object Page in Fiori List Report is blank (Column header and Data from CDS view not getting loaded)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have created two consumption cds views where each one is associated to the other cds view. They are activated properly and odata services are also activated.

I am sharing the code for the cds views below:

CDS View 1: zc_flights

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCFLIGHTS'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption view for Flights'
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
@OData.publish: true
define view zc_flights as select from zi_flights as consumptionFlights
association [0..*] to zc_flightbooking as _consumptionFlightBooking
on  consumptionFlights.Airline = _consumptionFlightBooking.Airline
and consumptionFlights.FlightNumber = _consumptionFlightBooking.FlightNumber
and consumptionFlights.FlightDate = _consumptionFlightBooking.FlightDate
{   
    .lineItem.position: 1
    @EndUserText.label: 'Airline'
    key Airline,
    .lineItem.position: 2
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Number'
    key FlightNumber,
    .lineItem.position: 3
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Date'
    key FlightDate,
    .lineItem.position: 4
    @EndUserText.label: 'Plane Type'
    PlaneType,
    .lineItem.position: 5
    @EndUserText.label: 'From City'
    CityFrom,
    .lineItem.position: 6
    @EndUserText.label: 'To City'
    CityTo,
    /* Associations */
    //_compositeFlightBooking,
    _consumptionFlightBooking
    
}

 

 

CDS View 1: zc_flightbooking

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCFLIGHTBOOKING'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption view for Flight Booking'
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
@OData.publish: true
define view zc_flightbooking as select from zi_flightbooking as consumptionFlightBooking
association [1..1] to zc_flights as _consumptionFlight
on  consumptionFlightBooking.Airline = _consumptionFlight.Airline
and consumptionFlightBooking.FlightNumber = _consumptionFlight.FlightNumber
and consumptionFlightBooking.FlightDate = _consumptionFlight.FlightDate
{
    .lineItem.position: 1
    @EndUserText.label: 'Airline'
    key Airline,
    .lineItem.position: 2
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Number'
    key FlightNumber,
    .lineItem.position: 3
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Date'
    key FlightDate,
    .lineItem.position: 4
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Booking'
    key FlightBooking,
    .lineItem.position: 5
    @EndUserText.label: 'Flight Customer'
    FlightCustomer,
    .lineItem.position: 6
    @EndUserText.label: 'Customer Name'
    CustomerName,
    .lineItem.position: 7
    @EndUserText.label: 'Customer City'
    CustomerCity,
    /* Associations */
    //_compositeFlight
    _consumptionFlight
}

 

 

 

Now, I have created a fiori application with following settings:

Template: List Report Page

Main Entity: zc_flight (1st cds view)

Navigation Entity: to_consumptionFlightBooking (because of association to 2nd cds view)

Now, after generating the project and when I try to run the application, the object page corresponding to the first cds view works well in fiori. But when I navigate to the second object page by clicking on a line item, I only get a blank screen where both the data and column headers are not present.

Object Page corresponding to main entity (loads initially when I run the application):

Object page corresponding to navigation entity (after clicking on a line item):

My expected result is that I need the output from the second cds view to get displayed when I navigate to the second object page. I have already checked the associations in the cds view and they are working fine.

Also including some code from webapp files of the Fiori application:

index.html

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge">
    <title>Flight Booking App</title>
    <style>
        html, body, body > div, #container, #container-uiarea {
            height: 100%;
        }
    </style>
    <script
        id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
        src="resources/sap-ui-core.js"
        data-sap-ui-theme="sap_horizon"
        data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
            "project2": "./"
        }'
        data-sap-ui-oninit="module:sap/ui/core/ComponentSupport"
        data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
        data-sap-ui-async="true"
        data-sap-ui-frameOptions="trusted"
    ></script>
</head>
<body class="sapUiBody sapUiSizeCompact" id="content">
    <div
        data-sap-ui-component
        data-name="project2"
        data-id="container"
        data-settings='{"id" : "project2"}'
        data-handle-validation="true"
    ></div>
</body>
</html>

 

manifest.json

 

{
  "_version": "1.53.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "project2",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:lrop",
      "version": "1.12.3",
      "toolsId": "8eceb3b9-5057-4f33-99df-633c2d4b679b"
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [
            "ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS_VAN",
            "annotation"
          ],
          "localUri": "localService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      },
      "ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS_VAN": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/",
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "localService/ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS_VAN.xml"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.114.9",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "sap.ushell": {},
        "sap.f": {},
        "sap.ui.comp": {},
        "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
        "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "project2.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
          "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
          "refreshAfterChange": false,
          "metadataUrlParams": {
            "sap-value-list": "none"
          }
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [],
      "targets": {}
    }
  },
  "sap.ui.generic.app": {
    "_version": "1.3.0",
    "settings": {
      "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
      "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
      "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
      "showDraftToggle": false
    },
    "pages": {
      "ListReport|zc_flights": {
        "entitySet": "zc_flights",
        "component": {
          "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport",
          "list": true,
          "settings": {
            "condensedTableLayout": true,
            "smartVariantManagement": true,
            "enableTableFilterInPageVariant": true,
            "filterSettings": {
              "dateSettings": {
                "useDateRange": true
              }
            }
          }
        },
        "pages": {
          "ObjectPage|zc_flights": {
            "entitySet": "zc_flights",
            "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
            "component": {
              "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
            },
            "pages": {
              "ObjectPage|to_consumptionFlightBooking": {
                "navigationProperty": "to_consumptionFlightBooking",
                "entitySet": "zc_flightbooking",
                "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
                "component": {
                  "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": [],
    "archeType": "transactional"
  }
}

 

component.json

 

sap.ui.define(
    ["sap/suite/ui/generic/template/lib/AppComponent"],
    function (Component) {
        "use strict";

        return Component.extend("project2.Component", {
            metadata: {
                manifest: "json"
            }
        });
    }
);

 

zc_flights_cds_van.xml

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<edmx:Edmx Version="4.0" xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_COMMON',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_UI',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_COMMUNICATION',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Communication.v1" Alias="Communication"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_PERSONALDATA',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.PersonalData.v1" Alias="PersonalData"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_VALIDATION',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="Org.OData.Validation.V1" Alias="Validation"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_ANALYTICS',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Analytics.v1" Alias="Analytics"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../catalogservice;v=2/Vocabularies(TechnicalName='%2FIWBEP%2FVOC_HTML5',Version='0001',SAP__Origin='LOCAL')/$value">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.HTML5.v1" Alias="HTML5"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="../../../sap/zc_flights_cds/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema Namespace="zc_flights_cds_van.v1" xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm">
            <Annotations Target="ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS.zc_flightbookingType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.LineItem">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Airline"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightNumber"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightDate"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightBooking"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightCustomer"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="CustomerName"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="CustomerCity"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS.zc_flightsType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.LineItem">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Airline"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightNumber"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="FlightDate"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="PlaneType"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="CityFrom"/>
                        </Record>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataField">
                            <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="CityTo"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

annotation.xml

 

<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Communication.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Communication.v1" Alias="Communication"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZC_FLIGHTS_CDS" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

I am not sure why I am not getting the expected output. I humbly request some help on fixing the above issue. 

Thank you!

entry.title: SRM - Einkaufswagen Monitor - Operatoren aktivieren
entry.content:
High All,

I'm looking for a way to change the filter options in the SRM shopping cart monitor. Does anyone know if you can influence it. More specifically, I would like to enable the operators for more fields.

 

I would be very happy about your help.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Unicode not working with API Gateway Export PDF on Crystal Report
entry.content: Problem with downloaded report in pdf. I am downloading a document prepared in Crystal Reports of type DLN2 (Delivery note). The same report in SAP works correctly, but after downloading via API Gateway service, strange and unrecognizable fonts appear on the printout.

entry.title: Re: Unicode not working with API Gateway Export PDF on Cr...
entry.content:
Problem with downloaded report in pdf.

I am downloading a document prepared in Crystal Reports of type DLN2 (Delivery note). The same report in SAP works correctly, but after downloading via API Gateway service, strange and unrecognizable fonts appear on the printout.

entry.title: Search Help Exit with own Selection and Dialog to choose a Value returning blank values
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a TMG, choose "new entries", enter something in field Tablename and Fieldname, click enter jump to field VALUE and press F4.

Now my search help exit comes into the game: Here I get searchhelp name based on of the field which I have selected in TMG dynamically through get parameters. Now I'm using this search help to determine (that's not SH for field VALUE!) valid value list for field VALUE, display it and let the user choose a value. Now my problem is when I'm clicking of F4 for the field VALUE, it shows 2 rows(Valid values for the selected field are yes and no) but the values in both the rows are blank. Please refer attached screenshot. Can someone please help in resolving this issue. How can see the list of values when I click on f4. Here is the code I have written in search help exit.

 

IF callcontrol-step = 'SELECT' OR callcontrol-step = 'DISP'.

GET PARAMETER ID 'SHLP' FIELD lv_shlp_name. " Getting SH of the field selected in TMG
IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

ls_shlp-shlpname = lv_shlp_name.
APPEND ls_shlp TO lt_shlp_name.

CALL FUNCTION 'GET_DOMAIN_VALUES'
EXPORTING
domname = ls_shlp-shlpname
text = 'X'
TABLES
values_tab = lt_valuestab
EXCEPTIONS
no_values_found = 1
OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

CALL FUNCTION 'F4UT_RESULTS_MAP'
EXPORTING
source_structure = lc_src
TABLES
shlp_tab = lt_shlp_name
record_tab = record_tab
source_tab = lt_valuestab
CHANGING
shlp = shlp
callcontrol = callcontrol
EXCEPTIONS
illegal_structure = 1
OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

LOOP AT lt_valuestab INTO DATA(ls_valtab).
ls_record_tab-string = ls_valtab-ddtext.
INSERT ls_record_tab INTO TABLE record_tab. "Pupulating record_tab manually as above function                                                                                         module returning 2 blank rows in results tab
ENDLOOP.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Need to change Company Code after copying project in...
entry.content:
Dear Brendan, 

I noticed the underlying WBS elements still have the Responsible Cost Center of the source project. Those WBS elements only become visible after you click on Create. If I then try to change those Responsible Cost Centers (from another CoCo then the source project) I receive an error and I am not able to change these Responsible Cost Centers on the underlying WBS elements. 

Do you have a solution for that issue? 

Kind regards,

Vanessa

entry.title: Re: New Learning experience for mobile
entry.content: Thank you - this is so helpful. It was presented to us that it would automatically update and show the new experience if it was turned on in the web browser and if the toggle is allowed, it would also show in the app to allow users to toggle back and forth.

entry.title: Re: How to connect an HTTP OnPremise destination using Ba...
entry.content:
Hi,

 Maintain this below mentioned properties(mandatory with other properties) in destination along with "Proxy Type: OnPremise

 

Authentication: BasicAuthentication
User:
Password:
"

Properties:

WebIDEEnabled: true

SAP-Client:client ID(like 300)

html5.DynamicDestination: true

Then in BAS:

1. go to menu View->Find Command

2. In the dialog enter Consume SAP Service

3. Choose the UI folder

4. Choose My SAP System

5. Choose the relevant data source (destination)

or

right click on manifest.json -> Open Service Manager and add your service

Reject

Description For MiPyme bonuses, debit or credit, the bonus currency to be authorized must be equal to the bonus currency. As far as I understand. The selected document is a ND due to exchange rate difference in $, with related invoice in USD

Any possible solution?
Thank you

entry.title: Re: Advice processing SAP Digital Payment add-on.

The advice processing in S/4HANA will be initiated via the Digital Advice program. Use Fiori App Schedule Accounts Receivable Jobs to schedule the job template SAP Digital Payments: Advice Processing help portal link

 

Regards,

Bhawesh

entry.title: Re: QM- Results recording
entry.content:
Hi Caesar,

In the setup of the MIC there are some Control Indicators you could contemplate on:

- Characteristic Attribute-> Uncheck so number of non-confirming quantity is used as result instead of catalog value

- Single/summarized result: Result per inspected parts as total (one result for all) or results for each part (a result for each)

 

greetings, Herman

entry.title: Ignite Your Passion for Technology: Become an SAP Certified Enterprise Architecture Consultant
entry.content:
There’s no doubt: New technologies like Artificial Intelligence rapidly transform the way we work – and will continue to do so. We see this in our private life, where playing with image editing picks up speed, where we generate songs for our friends and family, or simply ask an AI to write a letter for us. But we also see digital transformation and change in the businesses we work in. Imagine being able to design, structure and fine-tune the backbone of an organization with new tools. It’s enterprise architects who are responsible for this task – and you can become one of them.  

Using SAP LeanIX to excel at your job 

To be an outstanding enterprise architect, you need to gain profound knowledge and sharp skills in core competencies and tasks. And of course, you want to be able to demonstrate them, too, to make sure (future) employers can have confidence in you. If that’s what you’re looking for, then look no further. SAP has got you covered.   

Check out our brand-new role-based certification SAP Certified Associate - Enterprise Architecture Consultant - SAP LeanIX. This certification validates that you possess enterprise architecture fundamental skills and core knowledge in using SAP LeanIX required for the consultant profile. By getting certified, you can demonstrate that you have an overall understanding and in‐depth skills to participate as a member of project team with a focus on enterprise architecture using SAP LeanIX. 

SAP LeanIX embodies an innovative take on Enterprise Architecture, allowing accurate visioning, planning, and implementation of IT landscapes. This tool suitably complements the enterprise architecture and can prove fundamental in successful transformation projects in organizations. 

Start with this free learning journey 

Getting started is easy. Just look at the new free, self-paced learning journey "Learning to Use the LeanIX Tool for Successful Enterprise Architecture" on the SAP Learning site. In just 5 hours, you’ll get a deep understanding of LeanIX features, its set up and will be introduced to not one, but two practical use cases. No matter if you are a data scientist, project manager, consultant, administrator, or architect – this course is for you.  

Open new professional opportunities for you with new learning and a sought-after certification. Build the skills you need on the SAP Learning site and maximize your learning experience by joining the SAP Learning Groups, to ask questions from SAP experts and connect with fellow learners. When you’re ready, take your certification exam and start shaping your industry's future!

entry.title: Re: API 「G/L Account Line Items - Read」 get Open document
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: Defense & Security: Organizational flexibility
entry.content:
Defense is an "industry of industries." Defense forces have their own hospitals, aviation, research and development, real estate agencies, and logistics committees. They also face new demands such as "gender equality," "reduced carbon footprint," "blackout resilience," and the broad field of "trust." Defense forces must continue to operate even when all other parts of society collapse... (sounds familiar since 2020?) Self-sufficiency, resilience, and sustainability are crucial for them, as they are considered the "last resort."

Strategic Customer Development Project (SCDP) Bundeswehr

A strategic partnership between the German Bundeswehr and SAP for:

  • Development of new SAP standard functions
  • Integration of new functions into the SAP standard
  • Shared development costs between the partners
  • The goal of the SCDP was to develop a new SAP Enterprise Add-On.

The key strengths of this component are:

  • Comprehensive representation of organizational structures for basic operations as well as missions and exercises.
  • Complete process chains from planning through implementation and execution to the conclusion of missions and exercises.
  • Provision of information and evaluation capabilities.
  • Integration of personnel and material information and resource management.
  • Organizational flexibility in ongoing operations: changes in organizational structures and resource requirements are immediately considered and implemented in downstream processes.

Offline capabilities enable functionality even during temporary communication disruptions with central systems, supported by a decentralized architecture designed for mobile application integration. Specialized mobile applications for military, police, and aid organizations are included with SAP xApp Mobile for Defense & Security.

  • Integration with standard SAP processes (finance, human resources, logistics, etc.).
  • Customized workflows and management of object statuses and authorizations.

The German Bundeswehr implemented comprehensive, integrated standard software to support all logistical and administrative processes.

It Included

  • Personnel and organization
  • Accounting and Controlling
  • Armament and logistics
  • Infrastructure and enviromental protection
  • Force planning
  • Individual Traning
  • Healthcare

Cooperation with Defense Interest Group (DEIG)

The charter of the Defense Interest Group (DEIG) defines it as a "user group without authority" to engage in economically binding commercial transactions with SAP, but with the mandate to draw SAP's attention to specific user requirements.

Below are the following online resources to learn more about the topics discussed in this blog. The actual links may change over time, but you should be able to find the mentioned pages by searching for the following:

  • Reference library for SAP Fiori apps - a list of SAP Fiori standard applications https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/
  • SAP application help for the SAP Fiori Launchpad href=https://help.sap.com/viewer/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/7.4.19/en-US
  • SAP application help for DFPS - the ECC/compatibility scope defense solution https://help.sap.com/viewer/83ae2fb47d3f45099dc6d121b0a7706c/6.18.15/en-US/1d94cf535b804808e10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: AI Continues where Analytics stops...
entry.content:
AI is a relatively new buzzword but there has been a lot of talk around data analysis and analytics for over a decade. Herein we will present a perspective that AI is a continuum of data analytics.

Large portions of data science and analytics originated in the field of statistics. On the other hand, AI emerged from computer science as the practice and science of studying "intelligent agents." 

AI can be looked at as conventional analytics with the added dimension of cognitive analytics. Where conventional analytics stops at the ability to be prescriptive, AI enables technology to mimic human cognitive function, most notably with the ability to make future inferences via self-learning feedback loops.

 

Conventional analytics focuses on information levels and extends to the extraction of patterns and predictions i.e. conventional predictive analytics mainly leveraged by business intelligence professionals. Taking this discipline to the next step to where the technology can display characteristics of wisdom is where we enter the realm of AI. By making inferences from raw data and producing automatic decisions, AI provides autonomy with wisdom thereby taking conventional data science to the next level.

 

AI and analytics can therefore be seen as on the same continuum rather than separate fields altogether. There should be a strong push for AI and data scientists to codify interdisciplinary techniques to educate stakeholders and deliver solutions to customers.

Closing thoughts:

AI is an extension of analytics. Where analytics stops at the inferential decision making process, AI pushes the envelope by displaying wisdom and autonomy. AI is grounded on the same algorithms which are core to data analytics but with additional algorithms at the root of machine learning based on classification, regressions and clustering. Only AI is more powerful on the unstructured data via its vision and NLP algorithms.

This blog serves as an open call for a unified approach to analytics and AI.

entry.title: Re: Sales Performance CDS Views for Comparing Plan and Actual Data

entry.title: Re: Remove the ability to delete a purchasing order in Fi...
entry.content:
Hi @carminamatyas 

 

The Catalog SAP_SCM_BC_MRPCOCKPIT_MC includes the restrictions Purchasing Document Type ,
Purchasing Group, and Purchasing Organization so yes , i think if you created a Role , includes this catalog  and set the these restrictions as Read only and not write then it should disabled the deletion option.

Screenshot attached from an internal system from the app Maintain Business Role of what a restriction might look like

If that doesn't work then perhaps using Screen Personas you can hide the delete button

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html

 

Best Regards

Enda

2.if not, Can SAP elaborate on why the debugger functionality cannot be removed.?

3.What is the debugger permission used for? 

Thanks,

Kumar

Laurens

entry.title: Circular Economy: Batteries Regulation and Why Latin America is key to energy transition.
entry.content:
The new EU Batteries Regulation, which has just come into force, is set to transform how batteries are produced, used, and recycled across Europe. This comprehensive legislation aims to reduce the environmental impact of batteries by ensuring they have a low carbon footprint, use fewer harmful substances, and rely less on raw materials from outside the EU. By promoting the collection, reuse, and recycling of batteries, the regulation supports the EU’s transition to a circular economy and enhances its strategic autonomy in raw material and energy supplies.

Targets for recycling efficiency, material recovery and recycled content will be introduced gradually from 2025 onwards. All collected waste batteries will have to be recycled and high levels of recovery will have to be achieved, in particular of critical raw materials such as cobalt, lithium and nickel.

 

Lithium: Here’s why Latin America is key to the global energy transition.

A crucial element in this transition is lithium, a key component in batteries essential for the energy shift towards electrification and renewable energy storage. Latin America, particularly the lithium triangle of Chile, Argentina, and Bolivia, holds some of the world’s largest lithium reserves. As global demand for lithium surges, driven by the rapid growth in electric vehicles and renewable energy technologies, Latin America’s role becomes increasingly significant.

  • Around 60% of identified lithium is found in Latin America, with Bolivia, Argentina and Chile making up the ‘lithium triangle’.
  • Demand for lithium is predicted to grow 40-fold in the next two decades due to the energy transition to renewable power and electric vehicles.
  • However, there are concerns about the sustainability of water-intensive lithium mining in Latin America and elsewhere.

The EU Batteries Regulation is intricately linked to the need for responsible and sustainable lithium sourcing. The regulation includes due diligence obligations for companies to identify and mitigate social and environmental risks associated with the extraction and processing of lithium and other critical raw materials. This aspect of the law is particularly relevant to Latin America, where lithium mining can have substantial environmental and social impacts.

Ensuring that lithium is sourced responsibly from Latin America aligns with the EU’s broader sustainability goals. It helps prevent the adverse effects of increased mining activities, such as water scarcity and social conflicts, thereby fostering a more ethical and sustainable global supply chain. The regulation’s emphasis on recycling and material recovery also means that valuable materials like lithium can be reclaimed and reused, reducing the need for new mining and lessening the overall environmental footprint.

 

The Solution: enhancing supply chain sustainability with advanced traceability features.

To meet these stringent requirements, companies can leverage innovative solutions such as the SAP GreenToken. The SAP GreenToken solution provides a robust framework for tracing sustainability attributes and demonstrating due diligence throughout the supply chain. By using blockchain technology, GreenToken ensures transparency and traceability, enabling companies to certify the origin and environmental impact of their raw materials, including lithium.

In conclusion, the EU Batteries Regulation and the role of Latin American lithium are deeply interconnected in the global effort towards a sustainable energy transition. By enforcing stringent standards and promoting circular practices, the EU aims to set a precedent for responsible battery production and usage, while leveraging Latin America’s critical resources in a way that respects both people and the planet. This collaborative approach is essential for achieving a green and sustainable future for all.

If you want to know more about the solution, I encourage you to read this article.

Feel free to contact me for further information!

 

Joaquín Carballo

Sustainability Principal

M +54 9 11 2311 6947 | LinkedIn

entry.title: Re: Create a simple db-less Middleware solution with CAP
entry.content: Note the CAP license terms, https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/developer-license-3.1.txt, it does not "permit mass data extraction from an SAP product to a non-SAP product, including use, modification, saving or other processing of such data in the non-SAP product"

entry.content:
Hi there,

With the newly release generative AI Hub SDK https://pypi.org/project/generative-ai-hub-sdk/ it is now possible to interact with the Gemini model. Please note that Bison/Gecko are not supported as of now.

Hope this helps.

Christian

entry.title: SAP BTP Global Account, Directories and Subaccounts
entry.content:
SAP BTP Cockpit

Start the SAP BTP cockpit

https://cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Big Picture and Overview

How you can structure your SAP BTP account

Go to Account Explorer

Here you can create "Directories" and "Subaccounts"

Possible structures are:

  • by country/region
  • by department
  • by project
  • etc.

Example

As a simple example I use a headquarter in Switzerland and a branch in Italy.

Now you can configure additional functions for the relevant areas.

entry.title: Is it possible to change the format of date type fields in CAP?
entry.content:
Hello, everyone.

I have a question regarding the possible formats that a date can have in CAP. It's possible to obtain a view like the ones shown below:

It only shows the months:

It only shows the years:

It only shows the month and year:

I would like to know if it is possible to show only the month and only the year? Without having the need to create ValueList with String type fields.

Regards!

entry.title: Re: Tablas de referencias cruzadas con Crystal Report
entry.content:
Gracias por la sugerencia.

Para ponerle en contexto ya tengo dos tablas de referencias cruzadas en el informe, una en el encabezado del informe y en el pie del informe. Ya había considerado su sugerencia poniéndolas tablas en la misma sección y suprimiendo el objeto por la condición, pero el inconveniente es cuando dejo ese parámetro vacío.

El problema en síntesis es que la tabla de referencias cruzadas que tiene el agrupamiento de fila de vendedores, no me permite dejar ese valor vacío y me muestra el siguiente mensaje.

Necesito saber ¿Cómo puedo hacer para resolver ese error?

Quedo atento.

entry.title: SAP Group Reporting - Different Consolidation Methods for Statutory and Management Consolidation
entry.content:
Good day!

We have client requirement to perform Statutory and Management Consolidation in SAP Group reporting but different Consolidation Method will be use such as;

1. Statutory Consolidation will be using Purchase and Equity Method.

2. Management Consolidation will be using Proportionate Method. (Data source will be ACDOCA)

Is this possible in Group Reporting to have different Consolidation Method to be used? If you may share the solution on how we may achieve this requirements.

Thanks!

entry.title: Data Federation in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Data Federation in SAP Datasphere Data federation is a powerful data management strategy that enables seamless integration and efficient utilization of disparate data sources. In this approach, we don’t consolidate information or eliminate other sources; instead, we leave the data where it resides and use virtualization techniques to provide a unified view 1. How It Works: o Data federation doesn’t move data around; it keeps it in its original location. o Virtualization magic happens: We create a unified view of data from various systems, making it appear as if it’s all in one place. o Federated queries allow easy access to information across different platforms. 2. Why It Matters: o Data Explosion: With data multiplying like rabbits, we need efficient ways to manage it. o Better Analytics: Federated systems enhance data integration and analytics. o Customer Interactions: Businesses can provide relevant info by connecting diverse sources without redundant copies. 3. Not Data Consolidation: o Data federation ≠ data consolidation. o Federated architecture respects autonomy while integrating data sources. Benefits SAP Datasphere SAP Datasphere, a comprehensive data service built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that enables every data professional to deliver seamless and scalable access to mission-critical business data. With the unified experience of SAP Datasphere for data integration, data cataloging, semantic modeling, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization, data professionals can now easily distribute mission-critical business data — with business context and logic preserved — across their organization’s data landscape Data Federation in Datasphere - Virtually access and store data from anywhere Designed to enable seamless integration and real-time access to data across various sources • It employs a virtual database approach, allowing users to create virtual tables that aggregate data from different sources without duplicating it. • SAP Datasphere’s data federation architecture is supported by tools like the SAP HANA smart data integration data provisioning agent and adapters like the MSSQL Log Reader Adapter • These tools help in federating data from platforms such as Azure Synapse into SAP Datasphere, thus leveraging its analytical capabilities through SAP Analytics Cloud • Data Federation avoids extracting the data from SAP S/4HANA • Remote Table Federation: First, you can federate data, where you leave the data in the source system and access it remotely in real-time • Model Transfer approach: BW/4HANA rich metadata is imported from BW/4HANA to Datasphere, while the data is being virtually accessed • The architecture also includes the Federated-ML or FedML library, which addresses challenges of data migration and replication. It intelligently sources data in real-time from data storages for building, training, and deploying machine learning models on hyperscalers without the need to replicate or migrate data. Conclusion Overall, SAP Datasphere’s data federation architecture harmonizes mission-critical data throughout the organization, providing a unified service for data integration, cataloging, semantic modeling, and data warehousing, thereby enabling a business data fabric architecture.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to call a fix URL from IW52 trasnaction (Adding a cutom button there)?
entry.content: Search either "call a Web service" or "display an HTML page".

Hi!!!

I would also like to know a solution, as I have this problem in an MRS project.

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects - Content/Repository/InfoObjects metadata analysis
entry.content:
Hi SAP BOBJ Experts!

Just thought of sharing information in a consolidated manner so that all of the SAP BOBJ developers / administrators who are dealing with questions like :

- How do we know, about # of reports in any SAP BOBJ environment

- How those reports are linked to Universes or Connections

- Which User Group has what type access level defined in the system

- How many report are scheduled and which are those reports? how much time those reports take to refresh on BOBJ scheduler etc..

As many other blogs suggest that there are ways to find this info from BOBJ CMS Database/InfoStore however since there are many options available, I thought of sharing consolidated list of options; so that it serves as a good repository of info about commonly used options and allows stakeholders to make an informed choice (of which option to go for).

Broadly speaking there are more than 5 ways (barring SDKs) to extract metadata from BOBJ repository, some of the most common ones are listed here : 

  1. Query Builder
  2. CMS System Database Universe & Reports
  3. SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform Support Tool (SAP BI PST)
  4. Audit Database & Universe
  5. Central Management Console (CMC)

Let's look at these options one by one in a bit more details : 

Query Builder :

One of the out-of-the box option available is to use an SAP provided web/HTML based free-of-cost (available with BOBJ user licenses) tool called “Query Builder” Tool to extract the information. This tool can be access via URL http://ServerName:Port/AdminTools . It allows a user to directly query CMS system database.

This option works well with SAP BO XI R2 version or higher.

Although this tool comes handy in extracting information from BOBJ repository (and it can give you all sorts of info like Reports/Universe/User Groups/Access Levels etc..) however queries could be a bit complex for new developers and the results that Query Builder generates are not very easy to slice and dice if you have a long list of InfoObjects in the result set.

I think following KBA provides much more details on this option : 3441809 - BI Query Builder ***Guide KBA*** | SAP Knowledge Base Article

CMS System Database Universe & Reports :

This approach requires BOBJ Administrator to create a “Connection” using CMS system Database access driver to CMS repository. Once the connection is established BOBJ Universe designers can create a universe on the CMS system database tables.

Once the universe is ready, one can create their own custom WebI reports (or Crystal reports) to meet the data extraction requirements.

This entire process to define a connection, Universe and Reports does take sometime however there are sample Universe and Reports package made available by SAP (and other third-party vendors) which can be downloaded for free and configured by BOBJ Administrators to achieve quicker turnaround.

Please note that you can use this approach only if you are on SAP BOBJ BIP 4.2 SP03 or higher.

2399962 - CMS database with new data access driver from BI 4.2 SP03 | SAP Knowledge Base Article covers the subject in much granular details.

SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform Support Tool (SAP BI PST) :

Surprisingly not so widely known yet very powerful tool : SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform Support Tool is a Java based utility; (a client tool provided by SAP that can be downloaded from link: https://softwaredownloads.sap.com/file/0030000000399242022  on any client system) .

This tool is available free of cost and the installation steps are quite simple. The best part is : There are a set of pre-built reports available inside the tool which comes very handy for the repository analysis.

Please note that multiple newer versions (or updates) have been released by SAP from the time SAP BI PST Tool was first released in year 2014. This tool works well with SAP BOBJ BI Platform version 4.0 and higher however please check PAM document for lower BOBJ BIP versions for compatibility.

2407767 - SAP BI Platform Support Tool *** Master KBA *** | SAP Knowledge Base Article covers the BOBJ PST tool in details and has references to multiple resourceful documents.

Audit Database & Universe :

Auditing can be defined as: The process of recording actions performed within the system based on user activity. Auditing allows system administrators to know: who is using the system, how they are using it, when they use it and what they do.

Some of the very commonly audited activities are:
     - User Logon/Logoff
     - Report/Document access
     - Report/Document Scheduling
     - Custom Access Level creation, modification, or deletion
     - Lifecycle Manager events (includes rollback and version management actio ns

Some of the commonly used Audit database platforms are:
     - MySQL
     - Oracle
     - MS SQL Server

If your BOBJ system has provision for auditing, then you can create a universe on top of Audit Database and generate reports based off the Audit database to extract repository information.

A package containing sample Auditing Universe and Auditing Reports is available for download from SAP Support Portal however the Audit Reports/Universes are not supported by SAP for any issues/bug-fixes.

2456458 - Auditing in SAP BI Platform 4.x *** GUIDE KBA *** | SAP Knowledge Base Article explains Auditing in a lot more details.

Please note that you can use this approach with SAP BO XI R2 version or higher.

Central Management Console (CMC) : 

Set of metadata that you may need can also be queried/found from Central Management Console (CMC: Admin Console for SAP BO BI Platform), however one needs to all around to collect this info and may a times information is not available at a granular level.

Some of the info that can be easily seen from CMC is:
         - User Groups & Users
         - Folders and reports inside them.
        - Security queries: allows you to check Access Levels
        - Relationship queries: allows you to check dependencies between Report/Universe/Connection
       - Instance Manager: allows you to check Scheduled Reports

Summary :

Based on above mentioned details, SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform Support (SAP BI PST) tool looks to be the best choice if you are looking for a way to reliably extract the information from SAP BOBJ repository.

Additional favorable factors include : it’s a tool that’s delivered by SAP (a trusted source), available free of cost and doesn’t require complex configuration steps. However, if your requirements are beyond what’s already available through SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform Support tool (or you are using a BOBJ version lower than supported by BI Platform Support Tool) then you may consider other options to fulfill your needs.

Disclaimer : All information presented in this blog ( and references shared) is based on my personal experience while working on SAP BOBJ upgrade/migration and Development / Production Support project across multiple projects/clients.

If there is a feedback/question, please do share in "comments"  so that the content can be refined further ( if there is a need). The whole intent here is to consolidate the information that is available in fragmented format and allow BOBJ developers/ administrators to get a quick understanding of most commonly used options ; if they are heading for BOBJ content analysis.

Happy Learning!

Cheers!

~ Ashish

entry.title: Re: .NET USING SAP HANA DB REAL_VECTOR
entry.content: The problem was solved by updating the SAP HANA DB 2024.2.17.

entry.title: SAP Process Intelligence Imports not separating Activities from csv excel file
entry.content: I have been manually formatting my imported .csv excel files (ServiceNow) before importing them to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. I have made my files in the same format that was given in the eLearning exercise excel sheets. I have been unsuccessful getting the Process Intelligence Investigation to separate the "activities" as the sample excel sheets have done. I can only get one "Activity" to show. Any assistance on this matter would be much appreciated.

entry.title: Buffer table exists with a different structure after structural changes to source table
entry.content:
Hi,

We are getting SAP datasphere replication error "Buffer table exists with a different structure" after we made structural change to a table that is already replicating data.

I have had a look at SAP note 3297105 , and it says that structural changes to source objects are not supported.

Is there a workaround solution in order to avoid doing initialization again.

Thanks,

entry.title: Deactivated workflow for journal entry but validation still required
entry.content:
Hello,

I have deactivated a workflow for journal entry verification.

However, when creating a journal entry in "verify journal entries", the entry is submitted and requiring a validation.

Do you know if there is any specificity or prerequisite to consider?

Thanks & Regards,

Mathieu

entry.title: Re: Search Help Exit with own Selection and Dialog to choose a Value returning blank values
entry.content: Please embed the screenshot(s) as below

entry.title: Re: TM - Output request - Sender Country Can't be Initial
entry.content: We have the same issue. We added the ordering address and made the changes to OPD, but it didn't help. I also don't understand the logic of this error message. Where does this country can't be initial, come from?

entry.title: Re: #Multivalue error in webi
entry.content: Add those additional columns in FOREACH context in the formula/variable.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to Poznań SAP Community group!
entry.content:
Hi,

My name is Rahul Gupta. I have 14 years of experience in Finance Accounting. I have used SAP as end user so far but now want to move to SAP consultant role. I have started to learn S4HANA Fico. Currently staying in Poznan, Poland and working with Gsk. 

I'm looking for some guidance from this group to achieve my goal. Kindly assist me how I can start to work on some projects with hands on experience but with domain knowledge and as end user. 

Thanks and Regards,

RG

entry.title: Re: Onboarding Dashboard (New) in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: H2 2020
entry.content:
Hello,

If I cancel a candidate from onboarding in the ONB dashboard, where can I find the candidate details? I did a search for them in EC as an inactive user and I do not see them listed there. Can someone let me know where to find the candidate?

Thank you

entry.title: TTA for SAP integrated with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Here are some questions about the usage of the Test automation Tool from Tricentis:

- I would like to know if there are already predefined (created) test scripts available in TTA, if well where can we find it? I already looked in TTA itself and I didn't find it...

- Can we use the Best practices from SAP and import it in TTA in order to automatically create the Test scripts from scratch ?

- If none of above do we always need to create a test script from scratch in TTA?

- Right that there are 5 Tricentis license in CALM, what are limitation on it?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Search Help Exit with own Selection and Dialog to choose a Value returning blank values
entry.content: Don't use a search help, don't use a search help exit, don't use ABAP code, just enter the domain values in the domain.

entry.title: Re: Calling a BAPI inside a BADI
entry.content: that question is pretty much nonsense, if I'm not wrong.

entry.title: How to set Flat Hierarchy with a switch when 'Show only leaves' is activated
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working in SAP Analytics cloud and I'm trying to create a switch that changes all the row dimensions' hierarchies to FlatHierarchy. The issue I'm facing is that if one (or more) of these dimensions has already the 'Show only leaves' option activated, my script does not work.

This is my script:

if (Switch_1.isOn()){
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension1",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension2",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension3",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
Table_1.getDataSource().setHierarchy("Dimension4",Alias.FlatHierarchy);
}

If all dimensions are in the 'All' hierarchy, when I click on the switch it works, but if I have already selected 'Show only leaves' in any of the dimensions it does not work and throws this message "hierarchyId is undefined when process member navigation filter:(...)" ¿Is there any way of doing this?

entry.title: Re: Change the tax code validity period in Define Tax Cod...
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm not sure what you mean with data migration. From my understanding, you must activate Time-Dependent Taxes (TDT) for a target country/region in your system first. Once TDT is active, you can maintain tax codes and tax rates using the Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases configuration activity. 

Best regards,

Anja Danneberg, SAP

entry.title: Work Center Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi,

I manage a workcenter that operates with a variety of configurations and arrangements. My goal is to streamline the scheduling process by designating Thursdays for processing jobs that include "head" in the material description. To achieve this, I need SAP to allocate time at that specific workcenter for these particular jobs exclusively on Thursdays. What steps should I take to implement this scheduling adjustment within SAP??

entry.title: I am getting the error : 'Artifact upload failed due to an unexpected error.'
entry.content:
I am getting the error : 'Artifact upload failed due to an unexpected error.' when I am trying to upload any zip file of iflow. I have downloaded one iflow in a zip format and from one tenant and trying to upload that same zip file to another tenant. Then I am getting this error. 

Can you please help me on that?

entry.title: Register Now – Q3 Recruiting User Group Meeting - Sept 26th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the option "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please Register Here to receive the event invite and login details.

Q3 Recruiting User Group Meeting

Thursday, September 26th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Recruiting User Group is customer led by Purdue and Nestlé. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Recruiting customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from Recruiting experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

Thursday, September 26th - 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Intent based navigation - open target app in p...
entry.content: i don't think you need intent based navigation

entry.title: Re: Currency Setting in S/4HANA 2021.
entry.content:
Hi Benard,

Exchange rate is picked based on translation date (WWERT). Without any enhancement, Translation date is equal to Posting date (BUDAT). But if you want different date as translation date for example document date (BLDAT) as translation date then please use BADI =FI_TRANS_DRIVE_WWERT with FM = FI_DERIVE_WWERT. Hope this helps.

BR,

Harihar Prasad Kewat

entry.title: add/ read attachments of freight order
entry.content:
Hey there!

How do I add attachments to a freight order and how do I read them?

in R/3 ECC attachments have been added via Generic Object Services (GOS).

It seems that in S/4HANA the administration of attachments works completely different to GOS...

Thanks for any hint or advice!

Cheers
~Enno

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi All

 

My Name is Blessing.

Single mom, mid career transition. Ethnic minority in remote area of the UK.

I am looking to transition to become an SAP Consultant and this is a great opportunity for me to upskill and grow.

entry.title: Re: SFTP Sender Adapter - Multiple Java Nodes
entry.content: Did you get an answer to this? We are also facing the same issue intermittently.

entry.title: Buchungskreisübergreifende Erlösführende Projekte
entry.content:
Hello,

 

we´re creating Revenue Relevant projects in Public cloud. It seems it is not possible to assign different Company Codes (respectively different cost center from different company codes). All are in one Controlling area. Is that not possible in Public cloud? I know that setup from on-Prem Systems.

Does someone have any experience with that?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Cross-company code revenue-generating projects
entry.content:
Hello,

 

we´re creating Revenue Relevant projects in Public cloud. It seems it is not possible to assign different Company Codes (respectively different cost center from different company codes). All are in one Controlling area. Is that not possible in Public cloud? I know that setup from on-Prem Systems.

Does someone have any experience with that?

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: add/ read attachments of freight order
entry.content: Generally speaking, S/4HANA continues working with GOS a lot...

entry.title: Functional Area Usage
entry.content: I am working on restructuring my organizations functional areas. Currently, they are used to identify a detailed program, which constitutes a specific phase or segment of operations. They are currently two-digit alpha-numeric codes. I am trying to restructure the FA to identify organizational missions and associated sub-goals. Is there a preferred way to do this? Currently, I am thinking on identifying a new element to house the current functional areas which offer the same functionality of as functional area. I have done some initial research on Financial Management Area as a potential solution since it is also in the FM module. Would this approach work? Are there any other suggestions on identifying mission and goals in the functional area vs the financial management area or vice versa?

entry.title: Re: Check change history for the customer master
entry.content:
Hi Johan,

there is an app Display Changes to Customers.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3074615/E

Regards,

Doreen

Best Regards,

Sri

entry.title: Re: MDG DQM:Employing rules in central governance & mass processing in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

"additional section" in the rule definition, for what is it used?

Something with central governance but I 'm not able to find a document explaining the use of it.

Could you help me?

 

Thank you

 

Regards,

Matteo

entry.title: Withdrawing stock from material with material movement 221 shows zero
entry.content: While withdrawing stock for material  through movement type 221 the amount is shown as zero. Reservation  has WBS.. Expected is that amount should be shown when withdraw is done.. Could you please clarify if the amount is impacted based by the WBS element
